headline: ASCO 2026 Data Underscore Johnson & Johnson’s Expanding Specialty Drug Story
summary: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) reported multiple milestone clinical results at ASCO 2026 in autoimmunity, oncology, and myeloma. New data included the first FcRn-blocker efficacy signals in systemic lupus erythematosus and promising results in Sjögren's disease. Phase 3 PROTEUS data for apalutamide plus hormone therapy around surgery in high-risk localized prostate cancer were described as practice changing. TECVAYLI showed statistically significant survival benefits in relapsed or refractory...

headline: 75% Upside For LLY Stock?
summary: Even without its metabolic business, management argues Eli Lilly (LLY) would be one of the fastest-growing pharmaceutical companies in the industry. This is not a single-product story. The company's immunology, oncology, and neuroscience medicines collectively grew by 160%, showing the depth of its R&D engine.

headline: Goldman Sachs, Johnson & Johnson, MetLife Named Among Newsweek’s World’s Greenest Companies 2026

headline: CereVasc raises $85m to advance hydrocephalus treatment
summary: CereVasc’s eShunt is an implantable system designed to treat normal pressure hydrocephalus by draining excess cerebrospinal fluid.

headline: How to Maximize Dividend Income in Retirement Before RMDs Change the Math
summary: At the 24% federal bracket, a portfolio throwing off $40,000 in high-yield dividend income hands roughly $9,600 to the IRS every year when those shares sit in a taxable account treated as ordinary income. For investors in the gap years between retirement and RMD age 73, that drag compounds quietly until required minimum distributions force ... How to Maximize Dividend Income in Retirement Before RMDs Change the Math

headline: Without a Roth, $60,000 in Dividend Income From These 5 Stocks Means You Owe the IRS $22,200
summary: At the 37% top federal bracket, a portfolio throwing off $60,000 in non-qualified dividend income hands the IRS $22,200 every April, before state taxes and before the 3.8% net investment income tax (NIIT) surtax that also applies at this income level. That is the cost of holding REITs, BDCs and other ordinary-income dividend payers in ... Without a Roth, $60,000 in Dividend Income From These 5 Stocks Means You Owe the IRS $22,200

headline: Why Social Security’s 2.8% COLA Isn’t Keeping Up With What Retirees Actually Spend on Healthcare
summary: The Social Security Administration (SSA) set the 2026 cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) at 2.8%, lifting the average retired worker’s benefit from $2,015 to $2,071 per month. That extra $56 sounds fine on paper. Then the Medicare Part B premium notice arrives, the Medigap renewal letter shows up, and the math gets tighter than expected. One retiree ... Why Social Security’s 2.8% COLA Isn’t Keeping Up With What Retirees Actually Spend on Healthcare

headline: Can Pure Dividend Stocks Replace a $110,000 Dual-Income Household Income? Here’s What It Would Take
summary: A combined household income of $110,000 is close to the national norm for a two-earner household. For a 56-year-old couple hoping to retire at age 60 and fund their lifestyle entirely through dividend income, that annual amount becomes the income target their portfolio must replace. headline: CereVasc, Inc. Announces Over-Subscribed $85 Million Series C Financing to Advance its Novel eShunt® System for the Treatment of Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus
summary: CereVasc, Inc., a clinical-stage medical device company developing novel, minimally invasive treatments for neurological diseases, today announced it has successfully completed the initial closing of an $85 million Series C financing. Piper Sandler Merchant Banking led the financing with participation from Johnson & Johnson Innovation – JJDC, Inc., Johnson & Johnson's corporate venture capital arm, and Medtronic, along with existing investors Bain Capital Life Sciences and Perceptive Xontogeny V

headline: Purna Pharmaceuticals Names Marc Verbruggen as Managing Director
summary: Marc Verbruggen Managing Director, Purna Pharmaceuticals Puurs, Belgium, June 04, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Purna Pharmaceuticals (“Purna”), a specialty contract development and manufacturing organization and portfolio company of Ampersand Capital Partners (“Ampersand”), today announced the appointment of Marc Verbruggen as Managing Director, effective June 1, 2026.
